steps.title: Spot the key reversal
steps.content: The passage asserts decarcinization is typically an “evolutionary dead-end,” but then presents frog crabs—a decarcinized group thriving in sediment for millions of years. This counterexample undermines the initial generalization.

steps.title: Draw the inference
steps.content: D correctly identifies that the crab form is not universally optimal and that ecological conditions sometimes favor decarcinization. A and B misread or contradict the passage, while C is entirely unrelated. That matches choice D—D is correct.
